Article Title: CBI searches at 17 sites in cases linked to ADAG
Article Text:
Poll How serious do you think the allegations against Reliance ADA Group are? Very serious Somewhat serious Not serious at all





NEW DELHI: Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) on Saturday conducted searches at 17 locations in Mumbai in connection with three cases registered against Reliance ADA Group companies, Reliance Telecom Limited, Reliance Commercial Finance Limited and Reliance Home Finance Limited and their directors.According to CBI, the searches were carried out at the residential premises of directors of the companies and offices of intermediary firms allegedly used for diversion of bank funds.The searches were conducted on the basis of warrants issued on May 8 by a special CBI court in Mumbai.CBI said incriminating documents were seized during the searches. Investigators also found that multiple intermediary companies were allegedly operating from the same address.The agency said it had registered seven cases against the Anil Ambani-led Reliance Group in recent months based on complaints filed by various public sector banks and LIC in connection with alleged frauds involving thousands of crores of rupees. The total alleged loss in these cases is Rs 27,337 crore, according to CBI.The CBI had earlier conducted searches at 14 locations after registering the cases. Two senior executives of Reliance Communications D Vishwanath, joint president, and Anil Kalya, vice president were arrested on April 20 in connection with the investigation. Both are currently in judicial custody.The CBI said the investigation into the cases is being monitored by the Supreme Court.
Article Summary: Cbi conducted searches at 17 locations in mumbai in connection with three cases registered against reliance ada group companies. The searches were carried out at the residential premises of directors of the companies and offices of intermediary firms allegedly used for diversion of bank funds.

Article Title: India's 1st-of-its-kind mandated GPS watch for 1L+ Hajis 'not working well', say pilgrims
Article Text: NEW DELHI: When a sudden airfare hike of ₹10,000 per pilgrim was announced in late April, it set off a wider debate over what India's Haj pilgrims were actually paying for—and one item that came under scrutiny was a ₹7,000 deduction for a GPS-enabled smartwatch that each of the 1.2 lakh pilgrims had been mandated to carry.The watch, procured by the Haj Committee of India and supplied by a Delhi-based firm, was positioned as a safety measure—a first-of-its-kind tracking initiative unmatched by any other major Haj-sending nation. But pilgrims who have since departed toldTOI "the device is not turning on, not holding charge, and not actually tracking anyone".On its part, the company in question told TOI that "the watches are fully tested, functioning, and delivering 1–2 days of backup despite continuous health monitoring and live location transmission on international roaming, and no hardware replacement cases have been reported".The 'Haj Suvidha Smartwatch' is a required safety device for Indian pilgrims this year. The decision, implemented by the Haj Committee of India, was centred around pilgrim safety, but has triggered questions over cost, execution and necessity.Each pilgrim has effectively paid ₹7,000 for the device, directly deducted from their Haj deposit—one of seven main cost heads in the standard Haj package including meals, accommodation, and conveyance.For families already bearing a total outlay that can exceed ₹4 lakh per person, and dealing with a sudden ₹10,000-per-head airfare hike announced in late April, the watch has become a flashpoint.According to members of the Delhi State Haj Committee, the smartwatch is included in the Haj kit distributed to pilgrims before departure: “Every year, some changes are made. The departure kit typically contains passport stickers, luggage tags, boarding passes, ID cards, and now, for the first time, a GPS-enabled watch,” a member told TOI.Haj is one of the world’s largest religious gatherings, with millions congregating in and around Mecca. Managing crowds, preventing stampedes and ensuring no pilgrim is lost are longstanding challenges. This year, 1.7-1.9 million people are expected to participate.Unlike India, other major Haj-sending countries such as Indonesia, Malaysia and Pakistan rely instead on traditional group management systems and mobile-based coordination.However, early feedback from pilgrims suggests that the rollout of the smartwatch that was designed to help track Indian pilgrims in real time and provide emergency assistance when needed, has been far from smooth.Usman Ansari, a pilgrim from Old Delhi, told TOI that many travellers—particularly elderly and less tech-savvy individuals—are struggling to use the device. “They told us the watch would be activated in Saudi Arabia, but most people don’t know how to operate it. Around 70% haven’t even been able to switch it on because some update or the other is required,” he said.
Article Summary: Haj suvidha smartwatch is a required safety device for indian pilgrims this year. The decision, implemented by the haj committee of india, was centred around pilgrim safety. But pilgrims who have since departed toldtoi "the device is not turning on, not holding charge, and not actually tracking anyone"

Article Title: Koli women chart new waters, set up seafood company
Article Text:
1

2





It's a Thursday morning, and three directors and several shareholders of a seafood company are gathered around a conference table, absorbed in a presentation on food packaging. "Good packaging adds value to a product; it can even fetch a higher price," says the instructor at the Indian Institute of Packaging, in Marathi.As the directors nod, the silver tinsel in the pechak-che gajre in their hair and the gold naths in their noses gleam. The Koli women have never attended a workshop. Nor have they ever worked for a company - let alone run one. Yet they are directors of Daryavardi Producer Company Limited (DPCL), Mumbai's first fish farmer producer organisation: a community-owned enterprise led entirely by Koli women that aims to transform their fish-vending trade from a cottage industry to a corporate venture.They already have 1,000 shareholders from the community. Besides delivering fish, masalas and snacks, they are diversifying into running live food counters and selling online. A community kitchen is up, a cloud kitchen is in the works, and exports are being explored.April marked three years since company's formation. "We didn't realise packaging affects shelf life; we always sold fish in polythene bags," says Pratibha Patil, a director from Juhu Koliwada."We've now learnt that packaging that is leakproof and attractive is not only more hygienic but can also help us sell more."For 600 years, Koli women have plied their trade as they learnt it: moving in a straight line from landing centres and wholesale markets to independent sales counters. Even as markets evolved and competition cut into profits, their model remained largely unchanged. But new currents are now coursing through the community, altering the way its women do business.And better packaging is only part of the shift. The larger picture includes streamlined operations, centralised logistics, product development, digital payments, soft-skills training, branding and marketing - all the functions of a modern business reshaping Koli commerce."We decided to follow the Amul model, bringing together Koli women to collectively produce and market fish and fish-based products. And because we wanted complete autonomy, we chose to run the enterprise not as a cooperative, but as a company," says Ujjwala Patil, a community leader who founded the fishers' rights organisation Daryavardi Mahila Sangh in 2018 - company's ideological forerunner. "What Amul did for the White Revolution, DPCL will do for the Blue Economy. "The company is an agglomeration of over 50 self-help groups anchored in koliwadas across the city, each carrying out specialised work that supports the larger business.
Article Summary: Daryavardi producer company limited (dpcl) is mumbai's first fish farmer producer organisation. The community-owned enterprise led entirely by koli women aims to transform their fish-vending trade from a cottage industry to a corporate venture. They already have 1,000 shareholders from the community.

Article Title: TOI wins top honours at INMA awards
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Times of India bagged the first and third spots in two categories, honorary mentions in three other slots at this year's International News Media Association (INMA) awards presented in Berlin on May 7.The first prize for Best Use of Print (Regional) went to "Thirukkural With The Times", a weekly column in TOI's Chennai edition that takes the wisdom of Tamil poet and philosopher Thiruvalluvar's classic to younger and English-speaking readers through simple, contemporary interpretations. Authored by writer Bharathi Bhaskar, the column shows how a couplet written over 2,000 years ago still holds relevance."I am Kolkata, Amar Para", a TOI Kolkata campaign, won third prize in INMA's Best Use of Social Media (Regional) category. It celebrates life in the city at whose core is the "para (neighbourhood)". The series comprised over 20 write-ups, each focusing on one para. One, for example, celebrated Boipara (or College Street).Honorary mentions came in three categories. One was the "Amar Para" series in the Best Idea to Encourage Reader Engagement (Regional). The other was Best Use of Video (National), where TOI Chennai's "The Keeladi Story" won plaudits. The third category was Best Revenue Diversification Strategy. In this, Bombay Times Lounge by TOI Metro Supplements was recognised for transforming celebrity-led editorial content into monetizable intellectual property (IP).
Article Summary: The times of india bagged the first and third spots in two categories, honorary mentions in three other slots at this year's international news media association (inma) awards. The first prize for best use of print (regional) went to "thirukkural with the times", a weekly column in toi's chennai edition.

Article Title: Cloudflare Layoffs : Cloudflare, whose CEO is said to personally send every offer letter company extends, is laying off 1,100 employees; HR memo says: In the last three months, our ...
Article Text:
Cloudflare CEO’s personal touch

Read the compete memo here

Team:

We are writing to let you know directly that we've made the decision to reduce Cloudflare's workforce by more than 1,100 employees globally.

The way we work at Cloudflare has fundamentally changed. We don't just build and sell AI tools and platforms. We are our own most demanding customer. Cloudflare's usage of AI has increased by more than 600% in the last three months alone. Employees across the company from engineering to HR to finance to marketing run thousands of AI agent sessions each day to get their work done. That means we have to be intentional in how we architect our company for th eagentic AI era in order to supercharge the value we deliver to our customers and to honor our mission to help build a better Internet for everyone, everywhere.

Today is a hard day. This decision unfortunately means saying goodbye to teammates who have contributed meaningfully to our mission and to building Cloudflare into one of the world's most successful companies. We want to be clear that this decision is not a reflection of the individual work or talent of those leaving us. Instead, we are reimagining every internal process, team, and role across the company.

Today's actions are not a cost-cutting exercise or an assessment of individuals' performance; they are about Cloudflare defining how a world-class, high-growth company operates and creates value in the agentic AI era.

This is a moment we need to own as founders and leaders of the company. Matthew has personally sent out every offer letter we've extended. It is a practice he has always looked forward to because it represented our growth and the incredible talent joining our mission. It didn't feel right for this message to come from anyone other than the two of us. Rather than trickling out notices through managers, we will be sending emails to every employee.

Within the next hour, every member of our global team will receive an email from both of us clarifying how this change affects them. For those departing today, we will send this update to both their personal and Cloudflare addresses to ensure they receive the information immediately.

It's important to us that we treat departing team members right and in a way that exceeds what we've seen from other companies. We believe acting with empathy isn't about avoiding hard decisions but rather about how you treat people when those decisions are made. If we are asking our team to be world-class, we have a reciprocal obligation to be world-class in how we treat them. We are pairing the directness of these measures with severance packages that lead the industry. The packages for departing employees will include the equivalent of their full base pay through the end of 2026. Healthcare coverage is different across the globe, and if you're in the United States, we'll continue to provide support through the end of the year. We are also vesting equity for departing team members through August 15th, so they receive stock beyond their departure date. And, if departing team members haven't hit their one-year cliffs, we are going to waive those and vest their pro-rated equity through August as well.

We've asked the team to do this only once, as hard as that may be today. We don't want to do it again for the foreseeable future. By taking decisive action now, we provide immediate clarity to those departing and protect the stability of the team that remains. We are making these changes now because making smaller, repeated cuts or dragging a reorganization out over multiple quarters creates prolonged emotional uncertainty for employees and stalls our ability to build. It's the right thing to do; it's the honest thing to do; and it reflects the values of the company we are continuing to build.

Cloudflare started as a digitally native company built in the cloud. That allowed us to catch up to and pass companies that had a head start of years or decades but were slowed down by outdated systems and processes. As we've now become the leader, we cannot rest on the workflows and organizational structures that worked yesterday. We're confident that our reshaped organization will be even faster and more innovative as we continue building the future.

To those departing us: you've helped build the strong foundation Cloudflare stands on today. We have the utmost respect for your work and gratitude for the impact you have made. We're confident you will land at other great places and build many future great companies, bringing with you a unique set of skills learned while building Cloudflare.

Transparency is a core principle at Cloudflare, and it was important that you hear this from us first. We will be heading to our earnings conference call at 2 PM PT, when we'll share more. We also plan to address today's announcements live with the team at our all-hands meeting.

It's not an easy day, but it's the right decision. Our mission to help build a better Internet is more important now than ever, and there's a lot of work left to be done.





Cloudflare is cutting more than 1,100 jobs across the world, according to a memo sent to staff on May 7. The cybersecurity company said that the layoffs are part of a sweeping reorganisation to prepare for what it calls the agentic AI era. As reported by Business Insider, the layoff announcement was made by the company just hours after it reported first-quarter earnings that bear Wall Street expectations, though shares fell more than 14% in after-hours trading. The executives noted that Cloudflare’s internal use of the AI has surged over 600% in the past three months with employees across engineering, HR, finance, and marketing running thousands of AI agent sessions daily.The memo explained that the company must rethink its structure to move faster and deliver more value to customers. “The way we work at Cloudflare has fundamentally changed. We don’t just build and sell AI tools and platforms. We are our own most demanding customer,” the leadership team wrote.The memo also highlighted that Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince has personally sent every offer letter the company has extended, a practice he viewed as symbolic of Cloudflare’s growth and talent acquisition.Prince and co-founder Michelle Zatlyn said it was important that the layoff message come directly from them, rather than trickling down through managers.Cloudflare also stressed that the layoffs are not about cost-cutting or employee performance but about reimagining processes and roles to align with AI-driven workflows. Departing employees will receive severance packages through the end of 2026, extended healthcare coverage, and vested equity through August 15 benefits described as “industry-leading.”
Article Summary: Cloudflare's usage of ai has increased by more than 600% in the last three months alone. Employees across the company from engineering to hr to finance to marketing run thousands of ai agent sessions each day. Cloudflare is cutting more than 1,100 jobs across the world, according to a memo sent to staff on may 7.

Article Title: JP Morgan: How the former JP Morgan banker ‘rehearsed’ sexual allegations against his boss by seeking ‘advice’ from an online legal chatbot, 'chats revealed'
Article Text:
Watch JPMorgan Executive Sued Over Sexual Abuse, Coercion And Racial Harassment Allegations In New York JPMorgan Executive Sued Over Sexual Abuse, Coercion And Racial Harassment Allegations In New York

Leaked chats of former JP Morgan banker

ASK A LAWYER

on-call

Ask a lawyer / Ask an Employment Law expert

I was raped, sexually assulted, harassed, and forced to do drugs by my former boss at Morgan Stanley. I was then

Expert's Assistant chat

Customer: Hi

JA: Hello! I'm here to assist you. Could you please share a bit about the legal issue you're facing?

Customer: I was raped, secually assulted, harassed, and forced to do drugs by my former boss at Morgan Stanley. I was then retaliated against for seeking to move groups internally at the firm

JA: I'm truly sorry to hear about your experience. It's important to address this situation. Could you let me know if you've already reported these incidents to any authorities or HR at Morgan Stanley?

Customer: HR conducted an "investigation" but they ultimately made me signed a Separation Agreement

JA: Thank you for sharing that. Did the Separation Agreement include any clauses about waiving your rights to pursue further legal action or discuss the matter publicly?

Customer: yes but I was forced to sign under duress

JA: I understand. It's important to address the circumstances under which you signed the agreement. Have you gathered any evidence or documentation related to the duress or the incidents you mentioned?

Customer: YES I am AMPLE evidence

JA: Thank you for letting me know. Having evidence can be crucial. Have you already consulted with any legal professionals about your situation?

Customer: no

JA: I'm truly sorry to hear about the distressing experiences you've faced; could you please provide the zip code where these events occurred, as it will help us better understand the legal context of your situation?

Customer: 90012

JA: Thank you. I need some further details to help the Lawyer. Can I have your full name?

Customer: Chirayu Rana Please - I need help

JA: Thanks! What's the best email for you?

Customer: @.*

JA: Got it. And could I get your phone number?

Customer: (***) ###-####

JA: OK. Thanks for the info. The Lawyer can help with your case against your former employer. And, how would you like to connect with the Lawyer - phone call or online chat?

Customer: phone call would now work?

Answered by Joycelaw in 5 mins • 10 months ago

Joycelaw

Owner of Law Firm

7634 Satisfied customers

Joycelaw, Expert

Hello! I'm ******, an attorney with JustAnswer. How are you doing today? I hope you're well. I understand you have some serious concerns regarding your former boss and the situation you faced. I'm here to help you navigate through this.

Customer

I'm doing fine, thank you. It's been a tough time for me, though. I was sexually assaulted and harassed by my former boss, and he even forced me to do drugs. It’s been really hard to deal with.

Joycelaw, Expert

I'm truly sorry to hear that. Have you reported this incident to the police?

Customer

No, I haven't. He threatened me while I was living in ****, and it made me really scared. I was working on ****, and it’s a small world there. I moved to **** for this job, and when he found out I was looking to transfer to another group, he became hostile. I recorded all our phone calls, so I have plenty of evidence.

Joycelaw, Expert

That sounds incredibly difficult. You mentioned signing a separation agreement; can you tell me more about that?

Customer

Yes, I signed it because I was scared and felt I had no choice. He said he would ruin my career if I didn’t comply. After I signed, he started spreading lies about me, claiming I made everything up. It was disheartening because most people at work didn't know the truth.

Joycelaw, Expert

I understand how frustrating that must be. Did you seek any counseling or support after these incidents?

Customer

I've been struggling mentally since then. It was a terrible experience, and I left under awful circumstances. He told everyone a false narrative about me, which made it even harder.

Joycelaw, Expert

It's important to address those mental health impacts. Since this happened in 2020, you might still have options. Have you considered filing a workers' compensation claim?

Customer

I didn’t think about that. I just feel stuck because I signed that waiver. Everyone seems to refuse to help me because of it.





A former JP Morgan employee has accused Lorna Hajdini, a senior bank executive of sexual assault. According to new findings by the New York Post, Chirayu Rana allegedly discussed similar claims with an online legal chatbot nearly 10 months ago. Rana was identified as the user behind public conversations on the website AskALawyerOnCall.com, where he allegedly described being sexually assaulted, harassed and forced to take drugs by a former boss.According to the report, the chatbot conversation took place around July last year and were publicly visible on the legal-advice platform. In the exchanges, the user identifying himself as Rana allegedly claimed he had been “raped, sexually assaulted, harassed, and forced to do drugs” by a former boss at Morgan Stanley. The conversation also reportedly included discussions about separation agreements, legal waivers and possible legal action.The Post also shared screenshots of the alleged chats which further revealed that the user (Rana) was connected to a person identified as “Joycelaw,” who claimed to be an expert in employment and business law. During those conversations, the alleged abuser was referred to as a man, which differs from the claims Rana later made in his lawsuit against Hajdini.Here are the leaked chats
Article Summary: Jpmorgan executive sued over sexual abuse, coercion and racial harassment allegations in new york. Leaked chats of former jp morgan banker chirayu rana. Chirayu rana allegedly discussed similar claims with an online legal chatbot nearly 10 months ago. Rana was identified as the user behind public conversations on the website askalawyeroncall.

Article Text:
What is NYT’s ‘Connections’ game

NYT Connections hints for today



Yellow: Informal ways to describe kissing

Green: Objects or symbols with five sides

Blue: Strange places to be ‘out of’

Purple: Phrases ending with candy brands missing the final letter

NYT Connections answers for May 8, 2026

Yellow: Most straightforward connections

Green: Moderate difficulty with clearer themes

Strategies for solving NYT Connections



Start with the most obvious groupings to simplify the board

Use elimination to identify remaining possibilities

Consider phrases, idioms and hidden patterns rather than direct definitions

Watch for misleading overlaps designed to create confusion

Other NYT games to explore



Wordle: Guess a five-letter word within six attempts

Spelling Bee: Create as many words as possible using a set of letters

Mini Crossword: A short daily crossword challenge

Strands: A modern word-search-style puzzle with thematic links





The New York Times continues to attract puzzle enthusiasts with NYT Connections, its increasingly popular daily word-association game that combines logic, lateral thinking and vocabulary skills. Today’s puzzle (#1062) features a playful mix of romantic slang, geometric shapes, idiomatic expressions and hidden candy-brand wordplay. While some categories appear straightforward at first glance, several misleading overlaps make the challenge trickier than expected. This guide breaks down today’s hints, full answers and useful solving tips.Connections is a daily word game from The New York Times that challenges players to identify hidden relationships between words. The puzzle contains four colour-coded categories: Yellow, Green, Blue and Purple, each representing increasing levels of difficulty.Players are presented with 16 words and must organise them into four groups of four based on a shared theme. These themes can involve meanings, phrases, cultural references, spelling patterns or wordplay, making the puzzle both accessible and deceptively difficult.Here are the correct groupings for today’s puzzle:Yellow: CANOODLINGFIRST BASE, MAKING OUT, NECKING, TONSIL HOCKEYGreen: FIVE-SIDED THINGSHOME PLATE, JEANS BACK POCKET, SCHOOL CROSSING SIGN, THE PENTAGONBlue: PLACES TO BE ‘OUT OF’LEFT FIELD, NOWHERE, THE BLUE, THIN AIRPurple: ENDING IN CANDY BRANDS MINUS ‘S’BURGER KING WHOPPER, FILM NERD, MEMENTO, PITCHER’S MOUNDThe game presents a grid of 16 words, and your task is to organise them into four groups of four.Each group shares a hidden connection that may involve meanings, cultural references, phrases or wordplay.A new Connections puzzle is released daily on The New York Times website and mobile app, giving players a fresh challenge every day.Study all 16 words carefully and look for the clearest connections first. Rearranging the grid can often help reveal hidden patterns.Select four words you believe belong together and submit them. The game will confirm whether the grouping is correct.Correct answers are colour-coded and locked into place, while incorrect attempts reduce the number of remaining guesses.You are allowed only a limited number of mistakes, making careful deduction and elimination important strategies.Difficulty levels explainedBe cautious of thematic traps, especially when multiple words appear connected to sports, geography or slangIf you enjoy Connections, The New York Times also offers several other popular puzzle games:
Article Summary: Connections is a daily word game from the new york times. Players are presented with 16 words and must organise them into four groups of four based on a shared theme. These themes can involve meanings, phrases, cultural references, spelling patterns or wordplay. This guide breaks down today’s hints, full answers and useful solving tips.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Steve Jobs: “We’re here to put a dent in the universe. Otherwise, why else even be here?”
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Steve Jobs (Image source: Wikipedia)

Quote of the day by Steve Jobs



“We’re here to put a dent in the universe. Otherwise, why else even be here?”



Why this quote continues to resonate with people



The mindset that shaped Steve Jobs’ vision



A quote deeply connected to purpose



The connection between creativity and impact



Why the phrase still feels relevant today



Steve Jobs’ life reflected both success and setbacks



How the quote applies beyond technology



The emotional side of ambition



How Steve Jobs influenced modern innovation culture



Other famous quotes by Steve Jobs



"If I were running Apple, I would milk the Macintosh for all it's worth -- and get busy on the next great thing. The PC wars are over. Done. Microsoft won a long time ago."

“Stay hungry, stay foolish.”

“Your time is limited, so don’t waste it living someone else’s life.”

"I'm the only person I know that's lost a quarter of a billion dollars in one year... It's very character-building."

"You have to trust in something -- your gut, destiny, life, karma, whatever. This approach has never let me down, and it has made all the difference in my life."

"I have looked in the mirror every morning and asked myself: 'If today were the last day of my life, would I want to do what I am about to do today?' And whenever the answer has been 'No' for too many days in a row, I know I need to change something."

A quote that continues to inspire generations







Some quotes are memorable because they sound courageous. Some live for decades, because they communicate an idea that people search for quietly throughout their lives. One of the most famous statements of Steve Jobs is in both categories. His quote, “We’re here to make a dent in the universe. “Otherwise, why else even be here?” resonates with entrepreneurs, students, creators, engineers, artists and dreamers the world over, as it speaks to ambition, purpose and the desire to leave a mark.It sums up an attitude that pervaded much of Steve Jobs’s life and work. He wasn’t interested in building a product to chase trends. Instead, he envisioned technology could be used to fundamentally change the ways people communicate, work, create, and live. That philosophy helped change companies, industries and consumer technology itself.”His ideas continue to shape modern innovation even two years after his death in 2011. The quote remains popular because it’s not only about business. It raises a larger question about human existence in general: are people meant to just go through life quietly, or can they make a meaningful difference?One reason this quote continues to be popular is that it taps into a common human experience.Most of us want our lives to matter for something. The scale may vary from person to person, but the desire to contribute, to create, to improve something or to leave behind meaningful work is very personal.When Steve Jobs said “put a dent in the universe” he wasn’t really talking about space or astronomy. The “universe” was the world around people. The systems, habits, industries and limitations that form life as we know it.The quote implies that people can shape that world rather than passively accept it as unchangeable.Students are often motivated by the statement because it encourages independent thinking and ambition. For professionals, it’s a reminder that work is more than just salary or routine. For entrepreneurs and innovators, it symbolises the notion that progress is often fuelled by someone who thinks existing systems can be improved.Steve Jobs was famous for more than making products. He transformed the way technology was presented to everyday people. He championed simplicity, design and accessibility at a time when computers were often seen as complicated machines for specialists.He’s been focused on creating products that feel intuitive and personal, from the Macintosh computer to the iPhone.Many people who worked with him said he was intense and demanding and very detail-oriented. Products, he believed, should do more than just work well; they should form emotional bonds with users.The quote about “putting a dent in the universe” is indicative of that larger ambition. It wasn't enough just to build another device. It was about making something that could change habits, industries, and communication itself.Over time, Apple products revolutionised the music, smartphone, animation, publishing and digital communication industries.The power of the statement lies in its association with purpose. Many people spend years trying to find out what makes their work or life meaningful. The quote indirectly says that contribution gives purpose.That contribution need not be global or revolutionary every time. For some, the process of creating positive change in their family, community, workplace or sphere of creativity may already feel meaningful.The quote is an invitation for people to think beyond a passive existence. It doesn’t just ask how to survive or get ahead financially, but what kind of legacy one wants to leave.There’s a reason why this statement shows up often in motivational speeches, leadership talks, graduation speeches, and entrepreneurship environments. It reflects a belief that human potential expands when people pursue work linked to meaning rather than fear.Steve Jobs, in his career, often spoke about creativity as a force that could change industries. He believed innovation happens where technology meets art, design and human emotion.This became particularly apparent in products like the iPod and iPhone. They were not just technological devices. They were also to be elegant, intuitive and emotionally engaging.The concept of “putting a dent in the universe” is therefore closely associated with creativity. It's the idea that creative thinking can change the way people live their everyday lives.Many historians of technology observe that the smartphone revolution changed communication habits worldwide in a relatively short period. Mobile internet, touch interfaces and app ecosystems transformed consumer habits, but also business, media, education and entertainment.That quote encapsulates the mentality that goes into that level of disruption.This quote may have been said years ago, but it is still relevant today as life in the modern world tends to pressure people to conform. Social pressure, competition for jobs, money fears and online comparison make it scary sometimes to take risks or the unconventional path.The quote is the answer to that fear. It’s about encouraging people to think for themselves and to contribute something different.It doesn’t mean becoming famous or building billion-dollar companies. Rather, it embodies the notion that human beings can shape the world around them by effort, creativity, persistence and vision.In many respects, it speaks to younger generations who are unsure about careers and identity. It’s a reminder that ambition doesn’t have to conform to traditional definitions.One reason people continue to quote Steve Jobs is that his own life had dramatic highs and lows. Not a smooth road to success was his career.He was fired from Apple, the company he helped start, in 1985. He later founded NeXT and purchased what became Pixar, which revolutionised animated filmmaking.Years later, he returned to Apple when it was floundering and helped lead its comeback with products like the iMac, iPod, iPhone and iPad.This background is why many people view his quotes as not just abstract motivational slogans but as reflections of actual experiences involving failure, reinvention, pressure, and resilience.The idea of “putting a dent in the universe” sounds more believable coming from someone who has tasted both rejection and great success.The quote is strongly associated with entrepreneurship and innovation, but its meaning goes much further than technology.Teachers may see it as life enhancement through education. Doctors may associate it with saving lives and promoting healthcare. Writers, artists, scientists, social workers and researchers might consider it a contribution to knowledge, creativity or social change.The quote resonates through professions because it’s about impact, not status.It’s this flexibility that is one reason the line still makes it into books, documentaries, classrooms, interviews and leadership discussions years after it was first spoken.The quote is also emotionally meaningful in that it recognises people want their lives to matter. Ambition is usually expressed in terms of money, recognition or achievement, but behind those goals there is often a deeper desire for significance.You can’t just “be.” You have to “do” something to be alive.Psychologists often point out the strong link between purpose and contribution and long-term life satisfaction. People usually feel more fulfilled when they believe their actions are making a positive difference to others or creating lasting value.That quote sums up that emotional reality neatly and memorably.Steve Jobs still gets referenced a lot by many startup founders and tech leaders today because he helped shape the culture of modern innovation. His influence helped make ideas like user-centred design, product storytelling, simplicity, and integrated ecosystems central to technology industries.Even competitors followed suit after the success of Apple’s products.By the 21st century, startup culture was closely tied to the concept of changing the world with technology. Not every company can do this, but the ambition itself is indicative of the mindset behind the quote.For many entrepreneurs, “putting a dent in the universe” became shorthand for building something meaningful, not just profitable.More than a decade after his death, Steve Jobs is still one of the most important figures in modern technology and business history. But many of his most remembered words are not about computers or devices. They are about purpose, about bravery, about imagination, about the human desire to build something that matters.We’re here to leave a mark in the universe. If not, why else even be here?” continues to ring true because it captures an idea that people naturally understand. Human life is more meaningful to people when they find something valuable to give to the world around them.The quote is a reminder that impact starts with the choice to believe change is possible, whether we do it through technology, art, science, education, relationships, or the simple daily acts of effort and creativity.
Article Summary: Steve jobs was famous for more than making products. He transformed the way technology was presented to everyday people. “we’re here to make a dent in the universe. Otherwise, why else even be here?” resonates with entrepreneurs, students, creators, engineers, artists and dreamers the world over.

Article Title: American billionaire Mark Cuban has a ‘message’ for OpenAI CEO Sam Altman: You are wasting a lot of …
Article Text:
Mark Cuban.

Mark Cuban questioned large AI models over profitability



Internal tensions at OpenAI



Mark Cuban’s advice to his daughter







American billionaire and former Shark Tank investor Mark Cuban has now taken aim to ChatGPT-maker OpenAI ’s business strategy, warning CEO Sam Altman that the company is ‘throwing ways money at scale.’ According to a report Benzinga, speaking on the Big Technology Podcast, Cuban expressed his skepticism about the sustainability of OpenAI’s massive spending on data centers and infrastructure. Curban has also argued that computing power is advancing so quickly and also becoming faster and cheaper that today’s eye-catching investment projections are unlikely to materialize. “The numbers thrown out there aren’t going to come to fruition. It’s not going to happen,” he said. He compared OpenAI’s approach to Apple, noting that Apple spent “next to nothing” yet built a foundation where developers could simply “plug and play” into its devices.OpenAI is valued at $852 billion and the company is reportedly preparing for a public listing, has failed to meet its revenue and new-user targets. The company missed its goal of one billion weekly ChatGPT users and annual revenue expectations, largely due to competition from Google’s Gemini. Cuban has also questioned whether large AI models like ChatGPT or Gemini will ever be profitable, suggesting the industry could mirror streaming with a few winners and many struggling or consolidate into one dominant player.Reports also suggest that OpenAI executive are prioritising cost control and disciple, sometimes clashing with Altman’s vision. CFO Sarah Friar has raised concerns about whether the company can afford future computing contracts if revenue growth stalls. High subscriber churn has added to the pressure, raising doubts about whether OpenAI can sustain its heavy infrastructure spending.Mark Cuban recently shared a ‘critical’ piece of advice that he once gave to his daughter. During an appearance at Big Technology Podcast, Cuban said that if he was graduating right now, “or if I was a 16-year-old looking for a job, I would learn everything there is to know about AI”. Answering the question “Who succeeds with AI,” Cuban replied saying “Whoever learns how to use the (AI) tools the best”. “It's like I was telling my daughter, if you learn how to use these tools, and you know how to think critically, you're curious, so you're always learning, you're always going to have a job because AI doesn't know the consequences of its action,” he said.He further stated that one must not be in a business where “you just trust the AI and it's like”, adding “Oh what did it do”. “You need somebody as the buffer to understand how, where, when, and why to or why not to use it. And that's critical. And that's middle management in a lot of cases,” he stated.
Article Summary: Mark cuban questioned whether large ai models like chatgpt or gemini will ever be profitable. He compared openai’s approach to apple, noting that apple spent ‘next to nothing’ yet built a foundation where developers could simply “plug and play” into its devices. Cuban also shared a ‘critical’ piece of advice that he once gave to his daughter.

Article Title: Mark Zuckerberg: Unhappy with Mark Zuckerberg's Meta buying AI startup Manus, Chinese government to startups: Reject funding from American origin companies, unless ...
Article Text:
Mark Zuckerberg, CEO, Meta

Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Manus AI that China has cancelled

China bans Manus AI founders from leaving country

Poll Do you agree with restricting US capital for Chinese AI startups? Strongly agree Disagree





The US-China trade war is back, and in full steam. There have been two parallel moves in the recent weeks, marking a significant escalation of the US-China AI war. In the latest move, China's industry regulator has asked Facebook-parent Meta to withdraw a $2 billion acquisition transaction of Singapore-based AI company with Chinese roots Manus. What makes this more interesting is that the offer to cancel the deal comes almost four months after it was announced, which is in December 2025. In a statement, the China’s National Development and Reform Commission said that the decision to prohibit foreign investment in Manus AI was made in accordance with the country's laws and regulations. It further added that the parties involved have been told to withdraw the acquisition transaction.Earlier last week, the US government announced that it would crack down on foreign technology companies, singling out China, that are "exploiting" US artificial intelligence (AI) models, a practice known as model distillation. Now reports suggest that China plans to restrict its leading technology companies, including top AI startups, from accepting US capital without first obtaining government approval.The Chinese regulators' plan is reported to be part of China's broader response to Meta Platforms' controversial acquisition of Chinese-origin AI startup Manus. According to a report in Bloomberg, Chinese agencies including the National Development and Reform Commission have told several private companies in recent weeks that they should reject capital of US origin in funding rounds unless explicitly approved. The report quotes people familiar with the matter. Moonshot AI, which is considering an initial public offering (IPO), is reportedly among those that got the guidance from China's powerful state planner. Fellow Chinese startup StepFun too is said to have received similar instructions. The report claims that Chinese regulators have also decided on similar restrictions for the country's biggest technology companies including ByteDance, the owner of TikTok and the most valuable startup in the country.As for the reason, the report said that the intent of the latest restrictions is to prevent US investors from taking stakes in sensitive sectors where national security is a priority. The twin moves suggest that regulators are worried about a leakage of homegrown technology abroad as Chinese-founded startups and companies explore international opportunities. In the wake of the Manus acquisition, many academics decried the loss of a valuable asset to the US. Many worried that the deal would encourage other startups to follow suit.Manus was dubbed as China’s next DeepSeek and the company claimed that its AI agent could buy property, program video games, analyse stocks, and plan travel itineraries. In December 2025, Meta announced that it would acquire Manus to boost its AI development. Meta acquired it for $2 billion. Manus was founded in China but in 2025 the company relocated its headquarters and core team to Singapore. Manus was then operated by Singapore-based Butterfly Effect Pte. Welcoming Manus buy, Meta's chief AI officer Alexandr Wang wrote on X that Manus’s 100-strong team strengthened the US tech giant’s expanding ambitions to build a large AI team in Singapore and “amazing” AI products.Shortly after the acquisition was announced in December, China’s commerce ministry said it would investigate whether the deal complied with local laws and regulations. In March, China blocked founders of the startup from leaving the country, this was months after Manus was acquired by Meta. According to a report in Financial Times, scrutiny of the Manus transaction highlights growing concern about what Chinese leaders have described as “selling young crops” to foreign buyers in strategic areas such as AI. There are also reportedly concerns that by moving out of China, Manus bypassed domestic regulation in ways that would encourage other groups to follow. Manus’s alleged FDI violations are also said to be related to Chinese reporting rules after its ownership changed.
Article Summary: China's industry regulator has asked facebook-parent meta to withdraw a $2 billion acquisition transaction of singapore-based ai company with chinese roots manus. The offer to cancel the deal comes almost four months after it was announced, which is in december 2025.

Article Title: Canvas Hacked: Canvas learning platform hacked, millions of students in US affected
Article Text:
Impact on universities



What the company about the hacking attack







The widely used Canvas learning management system was hacked on May 7, according to a report by Reuters. As per the report, multiple US college newspapers reported the hacking incident. The data breach also disrupted access to coursework, grades and exam materials during finals week, leaving students and faculty across thousands of institutions unable to log in.The incident, now being called the 2026 Canvas security incident, is an ongoing cybersecurity breach affecting Canvas LMS, a learning management system operated by Instructure. In early May, Instructure disclosed it was investigating a cybersecurity incident involving certain user data, including names, email addresses, student ID numbers, and messages among users. The company said it found no evidence that passwords, dates of birth, government identifiers, or financial information were involved.The report by Reuters also revealed that the hacker group called ShinyHunters claimed the responsibility for the attack, posting ransom notes on Canavs login pages. The criminal hacking group ShinyHunters, which reportedly formed in 2019 and operates on a "pay or leak" framework to extort companies and institutions, has claimed responsibility for the breach.On May 7, Canvas, Canvas Beta, and Canvas Test were placed into maintenance mode while Instructure investigated the issue. The ransom message left on Canvas pages read in part: "ShinyHunters has breached Instructure (again). Instead of contacting us to resolve it they ignored us and did some 'security patches.'"The group warned universities to negotiate by May 12 or risk having stolen data leaked. Reports also suggest that the hackers may have accessed records from more than 9,000 schools, potentially exposing names, email addresses, student IDs, and internal messages.Several prominent institutions reported outages:* Harvard Crimson said students were locked out of Canvas.* Daily Pennsylvanian confirmed ransom demands appeared on Penn’s Canvas portal.* Duke Chronicle reported widespread disruption across Duke University.* Other affected schools include UCLA, University of Nebraska, and University of Missouri system, highlighting the scale of the breach.Canvas parent company Instructure placed the platform into maintenance mode while investigating. The company said there is no evidence that passwords, financial records, or government identifiers were compromised, but it has not confirmed the full extent of the breach. Universities have advised students not to engage with hacker messages or click links displayed on login pages.
Article Summary: The widely used canvas learning management system was hacked on may 7, according to a report by reuters. The data breach disrupted access to coursework, grades and exam materials during finals week. The hacker group called shinyhunters claimed the responsibility for the attack, posting ransom notes on canavs login pages.

Article Title: Netflix unveils ‘VOID’, an AI model that can change a movie plot
Article Text:
Netflix

How Netflix VOID works







Netflix has unveiled an AI model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, Netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next, essentially altering the causality. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background. VOID goes a step further: after erasing an object from a scene, it calculates how the surrounding environment should realistically respond to its absence, and in the process it adjusts the behaviour of other elements in the frame accordingly.The model, called VOID, is a vision-language system designed to edit video footage with a level of contextual awareness that sets it apart from several existing tools.For example, imagine this scenario: A movie director has just wrapped up a multi-million dollar action sequence where the star drives straight into a fiery, head-on collision. The cars explode, debris scatters, and the scene is a wrap.But suddenly, there is request to change the scene: The character survives and drive off into the sunset instead. This would mean costly reshoots or millions spent on computer-generated graphics.Netflix’s VOID can alter the scene and digitally erase the collision, the second vehicle, the smoke, and the flying debris. It will then generate new, realistic footage of pristine pavement, making it look as though the main character simply drove down an empty, peaceful road.The AI’s ‘magic’ lies in its grasp of real-world physics. Let us understand this by a different example. If a video shows a person jumping into a pool and splashing water onto the deck, VOID can completely erase the person. It will then intelligently alter the water and the wet concrete, leaving behind a perfectly still, undisturbed pool as if nobody had ever jumped in.“To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes,” Netflix said.“Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods,” the company added.Netflix isn’t keeping this powerful tool locked behind studio doors, and made the VOID model available to the public on the AI platform Hugging Face, allowing anyone to download and use it.While there are already several video-altering tools on the market, such as Runway, DiffuEraser, and ProPainter, the Netflix team claims VOID is vastly superior, as per The Register.
Article Summary: Netflix has unveiled an ai model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Mckinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.
